text.title: Custom Error Types
text.summary: Manual error enum with Display/Error impl, From conversions for ? propagation, and thiserror macros.

rule.statement: Define a domain-specific error enum for each module or library. Implement std::fmt::Display and std::error::Error. Add From<OtherError> impls to enable ? propagation from underlying errors.
rule.rationale: Typed errors allow callers to match on specific variants. String errors lose the original error type and prevent programmatic handling.

rule.statement: Use the thiserror crate for library error types. It generates Display, Error, and From impls from #[error("...")] and #[from] attributes, eliminating boilerplate while producing the same typed error enum.
rule.rationale: Manual Display + Error impls are repetitive and error-prone. thiserror produces identical code with a fraction of the surface area.

example.note: #[from] generates the From impl automatically. The variant is populated by ? when a sqlx::Error is propagated.
